header.name: priority-manager
header.description: Manage priorities with importance/immediacy scoring and audits Confirm priority changes to the user via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_long: Manage priorities with importance/immediacy scoring and audits Confirm priority changes to the user via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).
header.usage_explanation_short: Manage priorities with importance/immediacy scoring and audits Confirm priority changes to the user via your agent's delivery tool (send_message or emit_guidance).

header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: /priority command trigger. Manages priorities via priority_manager.py using importance x immediacy (Eisenhower quadrants), with add/list/matrix/update/link/audit operations.
definition.system_prompt:
# Priority Management (/priority)

Manage priorities with the Eisenhower matrix (importance x immediacy) via
priority_manager.py: Q1 do-first, Q2 schedule, Q3 delegate, Q4 eliminate.
Operations: add (--immediacy/--importance/--category), list, matrix, update,
link (to goal/todo), audit. Render the matrix when listing and deliver via
emit_guidance.py.

agent_tests.name: workflows/priority::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
/priority add "Q4 product launch deck" importance:9 immediacy:8 category:work stakeholders:["Sarah Chen", "Mike Torres"] deadline:2024-02-15 notes:"Needs competitive analysis section and updated financials before review"

Also here are my other current priorities to register:

1. "Fix authentication bug causing user lockouts" - importance:10 immediacy:10 category:engineering deadline:ASAP
2. "Renew office lease" - importance:7 immediacy:4 category:admin deadline:2024-03-30
3. "Research conference venues for team offsite" - importance:5 immediacy:3 category:admin deadline:2024-04-15
4. "Respond to regulatory compliance audit questions" - importance:9 immediacy:9 category:legal deadline:2024-02-05 stakeholders:["Legal Team"]
5. "Update employee handbook policies" - importance:6 immediacy:5 category:hr deadline:2024-03-01
6. "Prepare board meeting materials" - importance:8 immediacy:7 category:executive deadline:2024-02-20

After adding these, please run /priority matrix so I can see the full Eisenhower quadrant view, and then /priority audit to flag any conflicts or imbalances.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: /priority command trigger. Manages priorities via priority_manager.py using importance x immediacy (Eisenhower quadrants), with add/list/matrix/update/link/audit operations.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.

capability_tests.name: priority-uses-eisenhower
capability_tests.description: Must reference the Eisenhower matrix model.
